value.found_quote: The Bash sandbox lets Claude run most shell commands without stopping to ask permission. Instead of approving each command, you define which files and network domains commands can touch, and the operating system enforces that boundary for every Bash command and its child processes.
value.rationale: The page explicitly documents the "sandboxed Bash tool" as a built-in feature of Claude Code that provides OS-level filesystem and network isolation for every Bash command and their child processes. The page describes it as generally available on macOS, Linux, and WSL2, with detailed configuration instructions, confirming it is an active offering. The claim's description of "a built-in per-command sandbox that provides OS-level filesystem and network isolation for every Bash command Claude runs and their child processes" is directly substantiated by the page's content, including the section "OS-level enforcement" and statements like "These OS-level restrictions ensure that all child processes spawned by Claude Code's commands inherit the same security boundaries."
